rationale: The citing paper note centers on defined-contribution fund assortment effects and decomposition of the 1/n heuristic, and its described prior-work anchors are Huberman & Jiang (2006) and Benartzi & Thaler (2001), not disposition-effect mechanisms. The cited paper note is about winner/loser stock-selling (disposition effect) in e-trading, which is topically distinct from retirement menu allocation behavior. Given only one bare DOI match with no in-text citation context in the raw evidence, this appears most likely an incidental/background mention.
